name: Italian Christmas Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup (1 stick) Butter or margarine, softened
1/2 cup (4 oz) Red Candied Cherries, Halved
recipeInstructions:
If possible, check the temperature of warm water with a thermometer. Sprinkle yeast over water in a large bowl, stirring until dissolved. Add 1/2 cup sugar, salt, 1/2 cup butter, 3 eggs, egg yolk, and 3 cups flour; beat with a wooden spoon or electric mixer until smooth, about 2 minutes. Gradually add remaining flour; mix in the last part with your hands until the dough leaves the side of the bowl.
Turn dough onto lightly floured board. Knead until smooth (5 minutes). Place in a lightly greased large bowl; turn to bring the greased side up. Cover with a towel; let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
Grease well and line the inside of an 8-inch springform pan with a 4-inch strip of buttered brown paper. Punch down dough; turn out onto lightly floured pastry cloth or board. Knead in raisins and candied fruits until well distributed (5 minutes). Place dough in prepared pan. Brush top with 1 tablespoon melted butter. Cover with a towel; let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until more than doubled in bulk, about 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. With a sharp knife, cut a deep cross on the top of bread. Brush with egg white combined with 2 tablespoons water. Bake for 30 minutes. Remove bread from oven; brush again with egg white mixture and sprinkle with sugar. Continue baking for another 30 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from pan; cool on rack. Makes 1 loaf.
